message:
fix(fp-frame): to_multi_index now handles Float64/Bool columns correctly

to_multi_index was calling the strict scalar_to_index_label helper
which only handles Int64/Utf8 and silently converted Float64, Bool,
and Null values to empty string labels. A Float64 column like
[1.5, 2.5] would produce labels ["", ""] instead of ["1.5", "2.5"].

Fixed by using inline conversion that handles all Scalar variants:
- Int64 -> IndexLabel::Int64
- Utf8 -> IndexLabel::Utf8
- Float64 -> IndexLabel::Utf8(v.to_string())
- Bool -> IndexLabel::Utf8(b.to_string())
- Null -> IndexLabel::Utf8("")

Added test with Float64 and Bool columns verifying correct labels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
